data.title: Small-cap stocks hit record highs as investors anticipate Fed rate cut next week. Russell 2000 up 1% at 2,535. Nuclear stocks rally after Nvidia CEO touts nuclear power for AI data centers. Fed cuts projected through 2026, with 90% likelihood of 25-basis-point reduction.
data.description: On Thursday, small-cap stocks in the Russell 2000 index achieved new record highs as investors flocked to the most rate-sensitive sectors in anticipation of an upcoming Federal Reserve rate cut. The market is nearly certain of a third consecutive 25-basis-point reduction next week, with futures indicating an almost 90% likelihood of this move. Forecasts suggest further Fed cuts extending to 2026, with markets already factoring in two extra cuts by June 2023 and assigning close to a 50% chance of a third cut by the end of that year. As of 12:50 p.m. in New York, the Russell 2000 had risen by 1% to reach 2,535, just below its late-October peak of 2,540. Conversely, large-cap stocks like the S&P 500 remained steady, while the Nasdaq 100 and Dow Jones experienced minor declines. Noteworthy was the notable upsurge in nuclear-energy stocks following comments from Nvidia Corp. CEO Jensen Huang, who emphasized the importance of nuclear power in meeting the substantial electricity demands of AI data centers. The VanEck Uranium and Nuclear ETF also saw significant gains. Further details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Storable Marine team members Daniel Cohen and Caleb Scillian given 2025 Docks Expo Young Leader Awards for innovation.
data.description: Two individuals from the Storable Marine team were honored for their role in driving innovation and modernizing marine operations. Daniel Cohen, the Enterprise Sales Lead, was recognized for his contributions. Storable Marine, a prominent provider of specialty property management software, made the announcement in Austin, Texas on December 4, 2025. This recognition highlights the team's commitment to advancing marine operations and signifies the company's dedication to progress in the industry.

data.title: REV Group (NYSE: REVG) to report Q4 2025 earnings on Dec 10, 2025, with a live webcast at 10:00 am ET. Details on company website.
data.description: REV Group, Inc. (NYSE: REVG) of Brookfield, Wisconsin, will unveil its Q4 fiscal 2025 financial figures on December 10, 2025, before markets open. An online webcast at 10:00 a.m. ET that day will delve into these results. To participate, visit the REV Group website at www.revgroup.com, navigate to the "Investors" section, and click on "Events." Presentation materials will also be accessible on the site.

data.title: IOTA Mainnet links with LayerZero and Stargate, bridging 150+ blockchains for improved cross-chain communication and asset transfer.
data.description: The IOTA Mainnet has successfully integrated with LayerZero and Stargate, establishing connections with more than 150 blockchain networks including Ethereum and Solana. This integration aims to improve cross-chain communication and streamline asset transfer processes, allowing for enhanced interoperability within the blockchain ecosystem.

data.title: Meme coins are transitioning from speculation to utility, with projects integrating features like gaming platforms and DeFi. Utility-based meme assets are expected to reshape crypto markets, exemplified by Floki expanding into a metaverse game and Shiba Inu evolving into a decentralized ecosystem. Floki operates on Ethereum and BNB Chain networks, while Shiba Inu offers staking, yield farming, and a Layer 2 blockchain. SHIB is now used for purchasing digital land, NFTs, and is accepted on online stores and gaming platforms, highlighting its evolution from meme token to utility asset.
data.description: Meme coin utility is on the rise in the crypto landscape, with projects evolving beyond speculation into legitimate trends. By 2026, these community-driven tokens are reshaping markets with gaming platforms and DeFi integration. For example, Floki has transitioned from a Shiba Inu-themed coin to a metaverse game Valhalla with FlokiFi for staking and swapping. Operating on Ethereum and BNB Chain, Floki aims for long-term ecosystem growth. Similarly, Shiba Inu has expanded to include ShibaSwap for staking, Shibarium for scalability, metaverse initiatives, and NFT collections, becoming accepted at online stores and gaming platforms. This evolution signifies a shift towards meme coins with tangible utility. Floki sets itself apart with its focus on fast blockchain infrastructure and low-cost transactions, positioning itself well for future success in the crypto space.

data.title: Airbyte updates improve data transfers for customers with faster speeds and predictable pricing, ensuring accurate and secure data for AI and analytics.
data.description: In San Francisco, recent Airbyte updates are making waves in the industry. The enhancements offer customers quicker data transfers and predictable pricing models. This allows for precise and secure data flow for AI and analytics purposes. These updates are geared towards improving efficiency and reliability, ensuring seamless data integration.

data.title: Bitcoin critic Peter Schiff challenged on stage to verify realness of a $70,000 gold bar by Binance founder. Demonstrates Bitcoin's verifiability advantage over gold stored in vaults or transported. Zhao argues programmable scarcity is essential as finance moves to digital.
data.description: At a Blockchain Week event, Binance founder Changpeng Zhao engaged in a playful interaction with Bitcoin advocate Peter Schiff by presenting a 1,000-gram gold bar from Kyrgyzstan valued at $70,000 and challenging Schiff to verify its authenticity on stage. Schiff was unable to confirm the legitimacy without specialized equipment, highlighting the trust required in gold transactions due to the reliance on intermediaries. The incident, widely circulated on social media, underscored Zhao's argument for Bitcoin's advantage in instant verification through its ledger system. Zhao emphasized the importance of programmable scarcity and transparent settlement systems in the evolving digital finance landscape, portraying verifiability as a key strength of Bitcoin over traditional assets held in vaults or during transport.

data.title: DAT companies adapting amid market changes. Explore evolution, challenges, and future prospects in crypto market.
data.description: Companies specializing in Digital Asset Treasury (DAT) are currently experiencing notable transformations as a result of shifts in the market. This piece delves into the development, obstacles, and upcoming opportunities for these firms within the cryptocurrency market. It provides insight into how they are adapting to changes and navigating this evolving landscape.

data.title: Salon Ecru in Roseville offers free R+Co treatment with any appointment in December.
data.description: In Roseville, California, on December 4, 2025, Salon Ecru, known as one of the top hair salons in the area, has revealed a special promotion for December. Clients who book appointments this month will receive a complimentary in-house R+Co treatment.

data.title: CES partners with Wounded Warrior Project in a commitment to community impact, enhancing support for veterans.
data.description: In Dallas on December 4, 2025, City Electric Supply (CES) unveiled a significant partnership through CES Cares, the social impact division of CES. This collaboration underscores the division's latest pillar and reinforces its dedication to community impact. The partnership showcases CES's commitment to social responsibility and its support for organizations like Wounded Warrior.

data.title: Grain Management appoints former FCC Commissioner Geoffrey Starks as Senior Advisor, enhancing expertise in digital infrastructure.
data.description: On December 4, 2025, Grain Management, LLC, a prominent private investment firm specializing in digital infrastructure for the global broadband sector, revealed the selection of Geoffrey Starks, the ex-Commissioner of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC), to a key position. This move signifies Grain's strategic initiative in the industry.

data.title: Reko International Group Inc. reports Q1 fiscal 2026 results: Sales down by $1.2M, net income improves by 336.4% to $384. EPS at $0.07.
data.description: Reko International Group Inc. (TSX-V: REKO) based in Windsor, Ontario reported its first-quarter results for the period ending October 31, 2025. Highlights include a decrease in sales by $1.2 million or 11.6% compared to the previous year. Quarterly net income saw significant improvement, reaching $384 which is an increase of $296 or 336.4% from the prior year. Earnings per share stood at $0.07, in contrast to $0.02 in the previous year. The company maintained a strong cash position after repaying a $2.7 million mortgage and investing $2.2 million in growth-related capital equipment.

data.title: Wolters Kluwer named leader in Gartner Magic Quadrant for financial planning software for 5th time with CCH Tagetik.
data.description: Wolters Kluwer, a prominent figure in the professional information and software solutions industry, was recently recognized as a Leader in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Financial Planning Software due to its exceptional performance within the sector. Their CCH Tagetik Platform stood out for its cutting-edge features, such as AI-powered predictive budgeting and planning capabilities that include anomaly detection, driver-based analysis, and forecasting tools. These advanced functionalities provide finance leaders with valuable data-driven insights, enhancing their ability to adapt quickly, improve accuracy, and boost transparency within financial operations. This accolade showcases Wolters Kluwer's commitment to innovation and excellence in delivering top-notch solutions for financial planning, setting a high standard for the industry.

data.title: AWS unveils Graviton5 chip for Amazon EC2 M9g instances at re:Invent, boosting performance by 25% with 192 cores and 5x cache.
data.description: In Las Vegas, at AWS re:Invent, Amazon Web Services (AWS) has unveiled the new Amazon EC2 M9g instances that run on the latest Graviton5 chip. These instances promise a notable performance boost, offering up to 25% higher performance compared to the previous model. The Graviton5 chip boasts 192 cores per chip and a cache size that is 5 times larger, enabling customers to efficiently scale up workloads and enhance application performance, all while cutting down on infrastructure expenses. This marks a significant advancement in AWS's hardware offerings, emphasizing enhanced performance and cost-effectiveness.
